definition.name: compact_convex_set
definition.definition: A compact convex set K in a locally convex topological vector space is a subset that is both convex (λx + (1-λ)y ∈ K for all x, y ∈ K and 0 ≤ λ ≤ 1) and compact (every open cover has a finite subcover, equivalently every net has a convergent subnet with limit in K). By the Krein-Milman theorem, K is the closed convex hull of its extreme points: K = co̅(ext(K)). State spaces of unital C*-algebras are compact convex sets in the weak-* topology (by Banach-Alaoglu). The face structure of a compact convex set K is a complete lattice under inclusion. Every point x ∈ K belongs to a unique smallest face F_K(x). Reference: Alfsen, Compact Convex Sets and Boundary Integrals, Springer, 1971.
